things.name: Dnipro Embankment
things.description: One of Europe's longest river embankments stretching 23km along the Dnipro River; a beloved promenade with parks, fountains and panoramic views of the wide river and islands

things.name: Dnipropetrovsk National Historical Museum
things.description: Major regional museum with over 200,000 items covering the Scythian and Cossack eras, the city's industrial history and WWII exhibits reflecting Ukraine's complex past

things.name: Yuzhmash Space Museum
things.description: Unique museum at the Yuzhnoye Rocket Design Bureau, one of the world's leading rocket factories; exhibits on Soviet space exploration and the Dnipro rocket programme

things.name: Potemkin Palace
things.description: Magnificent late 18th-century neoclassical palace commissioned by Prince Potemkin, favourite of Catherine the Great; one of the finest Baroque-Neoclassical buildings in Ukraine

things.name: Monastery Island (Monastyrsky Ostrov)
things.description: Largest island in the Dnipro within the city; a green oasis with walking paths, beaches and the ruins of a 16th-century monastery; popular with locals for swimming in summer

things.name: Dnipro Art Museum
things.description: Regional gallery with outstanding collections of Ukrainian art from the 17th to 21st centuries including works by Aivazovsky; one of Ukraine's finest provincial art museums

things.name: Transfiguration Cathedral
things.description: Classical 19th-century Orthodox cathedral and one of the landmarks of Dnipro's historic centre; the burial place of Prince Potemkin and a major centre of Orthodox worship

things.name: Karl Marx Prospect (Main Avenue)
things.description: Dnipro's grand central avenue lined with impressive Soviet-era architecture, shops and restaurants; wide and tree-lined, it reflects the Soviet-era industrial city planning

things.name: Gogol Theatre
things.description: One of Ukraine's leading academic drama theatres in an imposing neoclassical building; performances celebrating Ukrainian and world dramatic literature with a strong local ensemble

things.name: Olimpiyskyi Stadium
things.description: Home of FC Dnipro Dnipropetrovsk with 31,003 capacity; the club was 2014-15 UEFA Europa League finalist, reaching the final at Warsaw before losing to Sevilla
